monuments.name: Taj Mahal
monuments.description:
The Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum built by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal. Completed in the 17th century, it is renowned for its symmetry, intricate inlay work, and breathtaking dome and minarets.

Often described as an icon of love and architectural mastery, the Taj Mahal draws visitors from around the world and remains one of India’s most celebrated heritage sites.
monuments.tourDescription: Begin your virtual visit at the main gate, then follow the guide toward the central dome—notice how the white marble reflections change with the light.

comments.name: Aarav
comments.message: The symmetry is mesmerizing. The virtual tour explanation helped a lot.

comments.name: Meera
comments.message: Love how the guide highlights the dome details.

monuments.name: Qutub Minar
monuments.description:
Qutub Minar is a soaring minaret that stands as part of the Qutb complex in Delhi. Built over several phases, it showcases early Indo-Islamic architecture with layered stonework and inscriptions.

As you explore, focus on the subtle changes in texture and the way the tower’s form transitions as it rises.
monuments.tourDescription: Take a guided look at the tower’s progression—imagine how each historical phase contributed to its current shape.

comments.name: Zoya
comments.message: The guide text makes the architecture feel alive.

monuments.name: Hampi
monuments.description:
Hampi is a remarkable landscape of ruins that once formed the core of the Vijayanagara Empire. Its massive stone structures, temples, and market streets tell stories of trade, culture, and devotion.

In this virtual experience, you’ll explore the legacy of a powerful city—crafted with bold architecture and surrounded by dramatic rock formations.
monuments.tourDescription: Start with the skyline of ruins, then zoom into the temple architecture—notice how scale and geometry work together across the landscape.

comments.name: Kiran
comments.message: Hampi’s sense of scale is unbelievable.

monuments.name: Red Fort
monuments.description:
The Red Fort is a historic fortress built by the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan. Its distinctive red sandstone walls, grand gates, and ceremonial spaces represent the power and artistic vision of Mughal rule.

As you explore, imagine the daily rhythm of a royal capital—then focus on how architecture frames light, movement, and celebration.
monuments.tourDescription: Follow the guided path from the main entrance to the inner spaces, imagining ceremonies and gatherings within the fort complex.

comments.name: Ishaan
comments.message: The descriptions feel like a story.

comments.name: Neha
comments.message: Nice UI—easy to navigate between sections.

monuments.name: Konark Sun Temple
monuments.description:
The Konark Sun Temple is celebrated for its grand chariot-like design and exquisite stone carvings. Built as a symbol of the Sun God, the temple’s architecture reflects both artistry and devotion.

Though much of the structure has endured change over time, its carvings and layout remain a powerful testament to ancient Indian craftsmanship.
monuments.tourDescription: Let the guide lead you through the carvings—imagine the sun’s path as you move from base details upward.

monuments.name: Ajanta Caves
monuments.description:
The Ajanta Caves are known for their rock-cut architecture and stunning murals. Hidden inside a rugged landscape, the caves reveal stories through paintings and sculptural forms.

In this explorer, you’ll experience the visual journey—starting from the cave entrance and moving toward wall art that captures emotion and movement.
monuments.tourDescription: Begin with the rock-cut doorway, then follow the mural highlights as if you’re walking deeper into the caves.

comments.name: Dev
comments.message: The guided layout makes it easy to learn.

monuments.name: Sanchi Stupa
monuments.description:
The Sanchi Stupa is one of the most important Buddhist monuments in India. Its hemispherical dome, beautifully preserved gateways, and carved stories reflect the spiritual and artistic traditions of ancient times.

Explore the monument’s outer ring and gateways to understand how architecture and narrative sculpture work together.
monuments.tourDescription: Walk around the stupa in your imagination—observe how gateways and carvings add meaning to each side.

comments.name: Samarth
comments.message: This made Buddhist art easier to visualize.

comments.name: Tara
comments.message: The guide text is perfect for beginners.

monuments.name: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib)
monuments.description:
The Golden Temple is a landmark of Sikh faith, known for its radiant golden façade and serene reflections in the surrounding holy pool. Its open community spaces welcome visitors with a spirit of devotion and equality.

This virtual view focuses on the monument’s calm atmosphere and the way the surrounding water mirrors the golden structure.
monuments.tourDescription: Start near the holy pool and follow the guide inward—notice how light and water create the temple’s signature reflections.

comments.name: Kabir
comments.message: Beautiful atmosphere described in the tour guide.

comments.name: Aanya
comments.message: Great place to start exploring Punjab’s heritage.

quizQuestions.question: Which monument is located in Agra and was built by Shah Jahan?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was commissioned by Shah Jahan in the 17th century.

quizQuestions.question: Qutub Minar is located in which city?
quizQuestions.explanation: Qutub Minar stands in Mehrauli, Delhi as part of the Qutb complex.

quizQuestions.question: Hampi is in which Indian state?
quizQuestions.explanation: Hampi is located in Karnataka and is associated with the Vijayanagara Empire.

quizQuestions.question: Konark Sun Temple is located in which state?
quizQuestions.explanation: Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha.

quizQuestions.question: The Red Fort is located in which city?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Red Fort is in Delhi and is a symbol of Mughal-era grandeur.

quizQuestions.question: Which of these is a UNESCO World Heritage site mentioned in this app?
quizQuestions.explanation: Sanchi Stupa is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

quizQuestions.question: The Ajanta Caves are famous for which type of art?
quizQuestions.explanation: Ajanta is renowned for its murals and rock-cut cave architecture.

quizQuestions.question: The Golden Temple is located in which city?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Golden Temple is in Amritsar, Punjab.
